agents.name: Alert Correlator
agents.desc: Ingests Prometheus, Datadog and Dynatrace alerts. Deduplicates 3,200 daily alerts to 192 actionable incidents — 94% noise reduction.
agents.description: Correlates alerts across Prometheus, Datadog and Dynatrace for PACS, HL7, VNA and enterprise systems. Topology-aware grouping by AGFA platform service.

anatomy.process: Deduplicate → correlate by CI → score priority → route or suppress
anatomy.output: Enriched ServiceNow incident with blast radius map
anatomy.role: Alert noise reduction and incident creation
anatomy.expectedOutput: Deduplicated incident with topology context, priority and correlated signals
anatomy.decisionCriteria: P1 if: >3 related alerts AND patient-critical system. Suppress if: known noise >95% confidence.
anatomy.llmReasoning: Claude Sonnet for semantic similarity scoring between alert messages

chainOfThought.thought: 186 Prometheus alerts firing on pacs-app-prod-* — all heap-related. Most are duplicates of the same underlying event.
chainOfThought.action: Load alerts into dedup engine, hash by CI + metric + threshold
chainOfThought.result: 186 raw alerts → 1 unique signal — heap_used_ratio > 0.95 on pacs-app-prod-01

chainOfThought.thought: Single signal confirmed. Checking AGFA topology — pacs-app is patient-critical. This qualifies for P1.
chainOfThought.action: Apply topology graph: map PACS blast radius across hospital sites
chainOfThought.result: Blast radius: 6 hospital sites · 847 image retrievals at risk

chainOfThought.thought: P1 confirmed. Risk is high enough to page immediately. Creating ServiceNow incident with full context attached.
chainOfThought.action: Create INC0002101 in ServiceNow — attach correlated signals, blast radius, topology
chainOfThought.result: INC0002101 raised — P1 · pacs-app-prod-01 · routing to RCA Engine

agents.conclusion: Reduced 186 raw alerts to 1 actionable P1 incident. Blast radius mapped — 6 hospital sites. INC0002101 created with full context in ServiceNow.

agents.name: RCA Engine
agents.desc: Root cause analysis across PACS, HL7, VNA and enterprise systems. Traverses AGFA dependency graph — 91% average confidence.
agents.description: Traverses AGFA platform dependency graph correlating JVM metrics, DICOM throughput, HL7 queue depth and PostgreSQL replication lag into structured RCA reports.
agents.mem: Episodic — incident history + AGFA pattern library

anatomy.process: Traverse AGFA topology → correlate metrics → match Vector DB → score confidence
anatomy.output: RCA report with confidence score, causal chain, recommended fix options
anatomy.expectedOutput: RCA report: primary cause, confidence score, causal chain, similar incidents, fix options
anatomy.decisionCriteria: Confidence >90%: auto-approve remediation. 70-90%: HiTL. <70%: escalate to L3.
anatomy.memoryType: Long-term graph store — persists AGFA topology + incident relationships
anatomy.llmReasoning: Claude Sonnet for causal reasoning and nuanced correlation across DICOM/HL7/JVM signals

chainOfThought.thought: INC0002101: PACS JVM heap at 98%. I need to know if this is a code leak, a load spike, or a configuration issue.
chainOfThought.action: Query Prometheus: jvm_heap_used_ratio vs jvm_gc_overhead last 2h on pacs-app-prod-01
chainOfThought.result: GC overhead: 34% and rising. Heap growing 0.8% per minute. This is a leak, not a spike.

chainOfThought.thought: The growth rate is linear — consistent with an object reference leak. Checking DICOM receiver thread metrics.
chainOfThought.action: Correlate: dicom_receiver_thread_count vs heap_used_ratio (Pearson correlation)
chainOfThought.result: r=0.97 — strong correlation. DICOM receiver threads are holding object references.

chainOfThought.thought: Searching Vector DB for previous PACS heap incidents. This pattern looks familiar.
chainOfThought.action: Vector similarity search: embedding(pacs heap + dicom receiver + linear growth)
chainOfThought.result: INC0002065 match: 94% similarity — resolved by rolling restart + -Xmx12g patch. Same root cause.

chainOfThought.thought: Root cause confirmed: DicomReceiverThread object lifecycle leak. Confidence 91% — above auto-remediation threshold.
chainOfThought.action: Generate RCA brief + runbook — heap dump, rolling restart, config patch, code fix ServiceNow change
chainOfThought.result: RCA sent to Remediation Agent — risk score 18 — Sentinel path approved

agents.conclusion: Root cause confirmed: DicomReceiverThread.java object reference leak causing linear heap growth. Confidence 91% — validated against INC0002065 (94% similarity). Remediation plan: heap dump → rolling restart → -Xmx12g patch → ServiceNow CHG-8821 code fix.

agents.name: Remediation Agent
agents.desc: Executes approved remediation for PACS, HL7, VNA and integration systems. Risk-scored actions — autonomous below 25, HiTL above.
agents.description: Executes pre-approved runbooks via Kubernetes, ServiceNow and AGFA-specific APIs. Risk score gates determine autonomous vs human-in-loop execution.

anatomy.trigger: RCA brief received with confidence score and runbook
anatomy.process: Risk score → auto-execute or HiTL → validate recovery → close ServiceNow ticket
anatomy.output: Resolution evidence bundle + ServiceNow closure + change request if needed
anatomy.expectedOutput: Execution report: steps completed, health validation, ticket updates, MTTR
anatomy.decisionCriteria: Abort if: post-restart heap >80% OR any step returns non-zero OR health check fails
anatomy.memoryType: Short-term — retains execution state for current incident only
anatomy.llmReasoning: Claude Sonnet for dynamic runbook adaptation based on real-time health check responses

chainOfThought.thought: RCA brief received: DicomReceiverThread heap leak, confidence 91%, risk score 18. Steps 1-5 all below threshold 25 — auto-execute all.
chainOfThought.action: Parse runbook AGF-FIX-PACS-001, classify risk levels
chainOfThought.result: 5 auto steps queued — risk 18 · Sentinel path approved · no HiTL required

chainOfThought.thought: Capture heap dump first — forensic requirement before any restart. Non-disruptive, must not skip.
chainOfThought.action: kubectl exec pacs-app-prod-01 -- jmap -dump:format=b,file=/tmp/heap-INC0002101.hprof
chainOfThought.result: Heap dump 2.1GB saved — forensic evidence secured

chainOfThought.thought: Rolling restart — must be zero-downtime. One pod at a time, validate health before proceeding. 6 hospital sites watching.
chainOfThought.action: kubectl rollout restart deployment/pacs-app --strategy=RollingUpdate maxUnavailable=0
chainOfThought.result: Pod 1/3: heap 11% ✓ | Pod 2/3: heap 13% ✓ | Pod 3/3: heap 12% ✓

chainOfThought.thought: All pods healthy. Applying permanent config to prevent recurrence while code fix is developed.
chainOfThought.action: kubectl patch configmap pacs-jvm-config --patch '{JVM_OPTS: -Xmx12g}'
chainOfThought.result: ConfigMap updated — -Xmx12g applied · all pods restarting with new config

agents.conclusion: All 5 automated steps completed in 18m 22s. PACS recovered: heap 98%→12%, DICOM receiver healthy, 6 hospital sites restored. INC0002101 auto-closed. ServiceNow CHG-8821 created for permanent code fix.

agents.name: Log Analyzer
agents.desc: Ingests Elasticsearch and Datadog log streams for PACS, HL7 and integration systems. Identifies anomaly patterns and failure precursors.
agents.description: Analyses AGFA platform logs from Elasticsearch and Datadog — JVM thread dumps, DICOM receiver logs, HL7 message traces and Kubernetes pod events.

anatomy.trigger: Incident raised or proactive scheduled scan
anatomy.process: Pull log window → parse patterns → identify anomalies → report to RCA Engine
anatomy.output: Log anomaly summary with contributing signals and confidence
anatomy.role: Log intelligence and anomaly detection agent
anatomy.expectedOutput: Anomaly report: event cluster, frequency trend, confidence, source component
anatomy.decisionCriteria: Escalate if: event frequency >5/hour OR confidence >80% OR patient-critical pattern matched
anatomy.llmReasoning: Claude Sonnet for semantic log clustering and DICOM/HL7 pattern matching

chainOfThought.thought: INC0002101 raised. Pulling DICOM receiver logs from Elasticsearch for last 2h — looking for thread lifecycle events.
chainOfThought.action: Query ELK: index=pacs-prod-* component=DicomReceiverThread level=ERROR last=2h
chainOfThought.result: 2,847 log entries retrieved — 34 ERROR, 8 FATAL in DicomReceiverThread namespace

chainOfThought.thought: FATAL entries cluster in 4-minute windows. This is not gradual — it's abrupt failure. Checking thread lifecycle.
chainOfThought.action: Apply AGFA PACS pattern library — match FATAL cluster to heap leak signatures
chainOfThought.result: Pattern match: object retention in DicomReceiverThread finally block — confidence 89%

chainOfThought.thought: Log timestamps confirm thread failures started at exactly the point heap crossed 94%. Causal sequence clear.
chainOfThought.action: Correlate log timeline with Prometheus heap_used_ratio metric timeline
chainOfThought.result: Confirmed: heap 94% at 02:43 → DicomReceiverThread FATAL at 02:45 → OOM kill at 02:47

agents.conclusion: 34 ERROR + 8 FATAL events identified in DicomReceiverThread namespace. Pattern: object retention leak — confidence 89%. Causal sequence confirmed: heap saturation → thread failure → OOM kill. Findings forwarded to RCA Engine.

agents.name: Change Validator
agents.desc: Validates post-remediation recovery and auto-closes ServiceNow incidents. Confirms SLA status and triggers KB article updates.
agents.description: Validates AGFA platform recovery after remediation — checks all impacted systems, confirms SLA compliance and auto-closes ServiceNow tickets with evidence.

anatomy.process: Health check all impacted systems → verify SLA → close ServiceNow → update KB
anatomy.output: Validation result + ServiceNow closure + KB article update
anatomy.role: Change validation and post-incident closure agent
tasks: Check all AGFA blast radius systems
anatomy.expectedOutput: Validation result with health evidence, SLA status, ticket closure, KB update
anatomy.decisionCriteria: Close ticket if: all systems GREEN AND SLA met AND no new alerts in 5 min
anatomy.llmReasoning: Claude Sonnet for SLA narrative and KB article generation

chainOfThought.thought: Remediation Agent reports PACS recovery complete. I need to independently verify all 6 hospital sites before closing the ticket.
chainOfThought.action: Query Prometheus: DICOM retrieval success rate per hospital site — all 6 namespaces
chainOfThought.result: All 6 sites: DICOM retrieval 99.97% ✓ | JVM heap 11-13% ✓ | OOM kills 0 ✓

chainOfThought.thought: All sites healthy. SLA check — P1 target is 4 hours. INC0002101 opened 02:47, remediation complete 03:09. MTTR: 18m 22s.
chainOfThought.action: Calculate MTTR vs P1 SLA threshold (4h)
chainOfThought.result: MTTR: 18m 22s — P1 SLA MET (target 4h, achieved in 18 min)

chainOfThought.thought: Ready to close. Will attach full evidence bundle and trigger KB-AG-001 update with new pattern data.
chainOfThought.action: Auto-close INC0002101 in ServiceNow — attach heap dumps, metrics, timeline, agent log
chainOfThought.result: INC0002101 closed — evidence attached — KB-AG-001 updated with DicomReceiverThread pattern

agents.conclusion: All 6 hospital sites validated GREEN. MTTR 18m 22s — P1 SLA met by 3h 41m. INC0002101 auto-closed in ServiceNow with full evidence bundle. KB-AG-001 updated.

agents.name: Capacity Planner
agents.desc: Forecasts PACS storage, VNA capacity and AGFA platform resource requirements. Proactive recommendations before breach.
agents.description: Monitors DICOM archive growth, JVM heap trends and Kubernetes resource utilisation across AGFA platform. Generates capacity recommendations 7-30 days ahead.

anatomy.trigger: Scheduled daily scan + threshold breach detection
anatomy.process: Collect 30-day trends → project trajectories → identify breach risk → raise advisory
anatomy.output: Capacity forecast with breach timeline and recommended actions
anatomy.expectedOutput: Capacity forecast: at-risk components, breach timeline, recommended action, cost impact
anatomy.decisionCriteria: Raise advisory if: projected headroom <20% within 30 days OR current utilisation >85%
anatomy.memoryType: Long-term vector — retains 90-day trend history per component
anatomy.llmReasoning: Claude Sonnet for multi-variate trend analysis and natural language recommendation
metrics: Lead time: avg 8 days before breach

chainOfThought.thought: VNA partition /vna at 91%. Ingest rate 2.4 TB/day. At this rate, breach in approximately 4 hours. This is urgent.
chainOfThought.action: Query Prometheus: vna_partition_used_ratio last 30d — calculate growth trajectory
chainOfThought.result: Linear growth confirmed: 0.8% per day average. At 2.4 TB/day ingest, breach at 95% in 3.8 hours.

chainOfThought.thought: Also seeing JVM heap trend on PACS — even after today's fix, the 8g ceiling will be hit again without code fix. Need to flag proactively.
chainOfThought.action: Project pacs-app heap growth post-patch — model -Xmx12g ceiling vs current ingest growth
chainOfThought.result: At current DICOM volume growth, -Xmx12g ceiling reached in 14 days without code fix deployment.

chainOfThought.thought: Two capacity risks: VNA immediate (4h), PACS medium-term (14d). Raising advisory for both.
chainOfThought.action: Create ServiceNow advisory tickets CAP-AG-0041 (VNA) and CAP-AG-0042 (PACS)
chainOfThought.result: Both advisories created — VNA marked urgent, PACS marked high · attached to PRB-AG-00018

agents.conclusion: Two capacity risks identified: VNA /vna breach in 3.8 hours (emergency expansion required) and PACS heap ceiling in 14 days without code fix. ServiceNow advisories CAP-AG-0041 and CAP-AG-0042 raised.
